content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

Independently verified all three amendments against the code in src/adapters/fs.rs. Amendment dfea2d32 identifies a genuine inconsistency: line 2840 uses a function name as the `file` field in CorruptRecord while every other usage (~30+ instances) uses actual file paths. The two plan_hash backfill amendments (ecebda75, c54fc6aa) describe behavior that is intentional, thoroughly documented in comments, and correct — auto-population is deliberately avoided to prevent race conditions and ambiguous legacy entry relabeling. I agree with the planner positions on all three.

## Votes

- `fr-17-dfea2d32`: Accept
  Confirmed: line 2840 uses `file: "unique_terminal_replay_match"` (a function name), while all ~30+ other CorruptRecord usages in fs.rs use actual file paths like `milestones/{id}/milestone.toml` or `projects/{id}/project.toml`. This is a genuine diagnostic inconsistency. The function doesn't currently receive milestone_id as a parameter, but every TaskRunEntry in `entries` contains a `milestone_id` field (line 3054), so the fix can extract it from entries or the function signature can be extended. Low risk, clear benefit for operator debugging.
- `fr-17-ecebda75`: Reject
  The non-population is intentional and well-documented. Lines 3041-3049 contain a detailed comment explaining the dispatch-v1/snapshot-v2 race: if persist_plan() advances the snapshot between bead dispatch and record_task_run_start, auto-populating plan_hash from the snapshot would stamp the entry with a newer hash than the plan under which the bead was actually dispatched. This would cause false 'conflicting plan_hash' errors at completion time. The snapshot hash IS captured in `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` (line 3050-3059) for provenance, and `safe_plan_hash_backfill()` fills plan_hash later when provably safe. The amendment would reintroduce the exact race condition the code was designed to prevent.
- `fr-17-c54fc6aa`: Reject
  The behavior at lines 2608-2622 is explicitly documented as a 'deliberate deviation from Issue 3 acceptance criteria' with clear reasoning: when `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` is None, the system cannot distinguish between (a) entries created before any plan existed and (b) legacy entries from older code that didn't record the field. Backfilling in this ambiguous case risks silently relabeling a legacy entry with a newer plan version. This is the correct conservative choice — all NEW entries always set `snapshot_plan_hash_at_creation` via `snapshot_plan_hash()` (line 3050), so they are eligible for safe backfill. Only genuinely ambiguous legacy entries are skipped, and these will naturally age out.
